expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: heatmap highlighting effect scenarios cover activation, persistence, and reset behavior
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly includes sections and scenarios for: activation (e.g., "Highlight activation via tap on a single heatmap cell"), persistence ("Highlight persistence" with scenarios like persisting while staying on the dashboard and on rotation), and reset/clearing ("Highlight reset / clearing behavior" including clear by tapping empty space, toggling same cell, and navigation reset). execution_notes.md also states: "Scenarios explicitly cover: highlight activation, persistence, and reset behavior (benchmark expectation)."

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md is titled "# Phase 4a (Subcategory-only) QA Draft" and is organized as subcategory-first bullets under the feature area (e.g., "Heatmap highlight effect (iOS)") without introducing canonical top-level categories. execution_notes.md confirms phase4a contract checks: "Output is subcategory-first (no canonical top-layer categories like Security/Compatibility/E2E)."
